description: HubL variables available in HubSpot CMS templates

variables.name: account
variables.description: Company property values from a known contact's primary associated company. Disables page caching.

variables.name: company_domain
variables.description: Domain set in branding settings

variables.name: contact
variables.description: Contact property values for a known contact. Disables page caching.

variables.name: content
variables.description: Properties of email, page, or post content

variables.name: content.absolute_url
variables.description: Full URL of the page or post

variables.name: content.archived
variables.description: True if the content is archived

variables.name: content.author_email
variables.description: Email address of the content creator

variables.name: content.author_name
variables.description: Full name of the content creator

variables.name: content.author_username
variables.description: HubSpot username of the creator

variables.name: content.campaign
variables.description: GUID of associated marketing campaign

variables.name: content.campaign_name
variables.description: Name of the associated marketing campaign

variables.name: content.created
variables.description: UTC creation timestamp

variables.name: content.meta_description
variables.description: Meta description. Use page_meta.meta_description for pages.

variables.name: content.name
variables.description: Content name or post title

variables.name: content.publish_date
variables.description: UTC publication timestamp

variables.name: content.publish_date_localized
variables.description: Localized datetime using time zone from account settings

variables.name: content.template_path
variables.description: Design Manager file path to the template

variables.name: content.slug
variables.description: URL slug portion after the domain

variables.name: content.updated
variables.description: UTC update timestamp

variables.name: content_id
variables.description: Unique identifier for the content

variables.name: favicon_link
variables.description: Favicon source URL

variables.name: hub_id
variables.description: HubSpot account ID

variables.name: hubspot_analytics_tracking_code
variables.description: Analytics tracking code snippet

variables.name: local_dt
variables.description: Current time in report settings timezone. Disables page caching.

variables.name: local_time_zone
variables.description: Time zone configured in HubSpot Report Settings

variables.name: page_meta.canonical_url
variables.description: Official page URL for the canonical tag

variables.name: page_meta.html_title
variables.description: Page title for the <title> tag

variables.name: page_meta.meta_description
variables.description: Meta description for the meta tag

variables.name: page_meta.name
variables.description: Alias for content.name

variables.name: portal_id
variables.description: Alias for hub_id

variables.name: request_contact
variables.description: Contact data from the page request. Disables caching.

variables.name: site_settings
variables.description: Page, blog, and email settings

variables.name: year
variables.description: Current year

variables.name: standard_footer_includes
variables.description: Renders HubSpot tracking code and footer code. Required in all page templates.

variables.name: standard_header_includes
variables.description: Adds jQuery, CSS, viewport tag, and page metadata. Required in all page templates.

variables.name: builtin_body_classes
variables.description: Dynamically prints CSS classes differentiating content markup

variables.name: request_contact.is_logged_in
variables.description: Returns true if visitor is logged into private content

variables.name: request_contact.list_memberships
variables.description: Dictionary of list IDs for contact membership

variables.name: content.language
variables.description: Language settings with languageTag and textDirection

variables.name: content.language.languageTag
variables.description: Language identifier e.g. 'en', 'es'

variables.name: content.language.textDirection.value
variables.description: Text direction: 'rtl' or 'ltr'

http_request.label: HTTP Request Variables
http_request.note: These variables disable page caching when used.

variables.name: request.cookies
variables.description: Cookie names mapped to values

variables.name: request.domain
variables.description: Domain the page was accessed from

variables.name: request.full_url
variables.description: Complete page URL

variables.name: request.path
variables.description: URL path component

variables.name: request.path_and_query

variables.name: request.remote_ip

variables.name: request.scheme

variables.name: request.search_engine
variables.description: Search engine e.g. google, aol

variables.name: request.search_keyword
variables.description: Keyword phrase used in search

variables.name: request.headers
variables.description: Dictionary of request headers

variables.name: simple_list_page

variables.description: CRM object matching page request path. Null on listing pages.

variables.name: dynamic_page_crm_object_type_fqn

variables.name: node.pageTitle

variables.name: node.linkTarget

variables.name: is_in_hs_app
variables.description: True when rendering inside the HubSpot app

variables.name: is_in_editor

variables.name: is_in_global_content_editor
variables.description: True in the global content editor

variables.name: is_in_theme_editor
variables.description: True in the theme editor

variables.name: is_in_page_editor
variables.description: True in the page editor

variables.name: is_in_blog_post_editor
variables.description: True in the blog post editor

variables.name: is_in_email_editor
variables.description: True in the email editor

variables.name: is_in_previewer

variables.name: is_in_module_previewer
variables.description: True in the module previewer

variables.name: account
variables.description:
A dictionary that stores company property values from a known contact’s primary associated company. You can access specific company properties (both custom and default) via this variable by adding a period followed by the internal property name. For example, account.name would print the name of the primary company associated with the contact.

Using this variable will disable page caching. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives.

variables.name: company_domain
variables.description: Prints the company domain set in Website > Pages > Branding > Logo link.

variables.name: contact
variables.description:
A dictionary that stores contact property values for a known contact. You can access specific contact properties (both custom and default) via this variable by adding a period followed by the internal property name. For example, contact.firstname would print the contact’s first name.

Using this variable will disable page caching. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives.

variables.name: content
variables.description: A dictionary that stores various properties pertaining to a specific piece of content such as an email, a page, or a post.

variables.name: content.absolute_url
variables.description: Prints the full URL of a page, post, or web page version of an email.

variables.name: content.archived
variables.description: Evaluates to true if the page or email was marked as archived by the user.

variables.name: content.author_email
variables.description: The email address of the content creator.

variables.name: content.author_name
variables.description: The first and last name of the content creator.

variables.name: content.author_username
variables.description: The HubSpot username of the content creator.

variables.name: content.campaign
variables.description: The GUID for the marketing campaign that this page or email is associated with. This unique ID can be found in the URL of a particular campaign in the Campaign’s tool.

variables.name: content.campaign_name
variables.description: The name of the marketing campaign that this page, this post, or this email is associated with.

variables.name: content.created
variables.description: A datetime object for when the content was originally created, in UTC time. This variable can be formatted with the datetime filter.

variables.name: content.meta_description
variables.description: When pulling the meta description of a page, it is better to use the variable page_meta.meta_description.

variables.name: content.name
variables.description: The name of a post, email, or page. For pages and emails this will print the internal content name, while for posts this will print the post title. For blog posts, this is the post title that displays. For other types of content, this is generally an internal name. This variable includes a wrapper so that it is editable via the UI, when included in blog posts. If you want to print the content name without a wrapper, use page_meta.name.

variables.name: content.publish_date
variables.description: A datetime object representing when the content was published, in UTC time. This variable can be formatted with the format_datetime filter.

variables.name: content.publish_date_localized
variables.description: A string representing the datetime when the content was published using the time zone defined in the account’s default settings. This variable is also subject to the language and date format settings.

variables.name: content.template_path
variables.description: The Design Manager file path to your template (ie custom/page/web_page_basic/my_template.html).

variables.name: content.slug
variables.description: The URL slug of a page, post, or web page version of an email. This is the value that follows the domain. For example, in https://example.com/about-us, the slug is about-us. For the full URL, use content.absolute_url instead.

variables.name: content.updated
variables.description: A datetime object for when the user last updated the content, in UTC time. This variable can be formatted with the datetime filter. Does not equal content.publish_date on initial publish. Use the |between_times filter to test if a post has been updated after publishing.

variables.name: content_id
variables.description: Prints the unique ID for a page, post, or email. This ID can be found in the URL of the editor. You can use this variable as an alias for content.id.

variables.name: favicon_link
variables.description: Prints the source URL of the favicon. Learn more about setting favicons in HubSpot.

variables.name: hub_id
variables.description: The ID of your HubSpot account.

variables.name: hubspot_analytics_tracking_code
variables.description: Includes the analytics tracking code. This tag is not necessary, because standard_footer_includes, already renders the tracking code.

variables.name: local_dt
variables.description: A datetime object of the current time in the time zone defined in your Report Settings. Usage of this variable will disable page caching in order to return the current time. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives.

variables.name: local_time_zone
variables.description: The time zone, as configured in your HubSpot Report Settings.

variables.name: page_meta.canonical_url
variables.description: The official URL that this page should be accessed at. Usually does not include any query string parameters. Use this for the rel="canonical" tag. HubSpot automatically canonicalizes URLs.

variables.name: page_meta.html_title
variables.description: The title of the page. This variable should be used in the <title> tag of HTML templates.

variables.name: page_meta.meta_description
variables.description: The meta description of a page. This variable should be used in the “description” <meta> tag of HTML templates.

variables.name: page_meta.name
variables.description: An alias for content.name.

variables.name: portal_id
variables.description: An alias for the hub_id variable.

variables.name: request_contact
variables.description: A dictionary containing data about the requested contact. Not available in email templates. Using this variable will disable page caching. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives.

variables.name: site_settings
variables.description: A dictionary containing various page, blog, and email settings, including colors and fonts and email footer details.

variables.name: year
variables.description: Prints the current year.

variables.name: site_settings.company_city
variables.description: Prints the company city.

variables.name: site_settings.company_name
variables.description: Prints the company name.

variables.name: site_settings.company_state
variables.description: Prints the company state.

variables.name: site_settings.company_street_address_1
variables.description: Prints the company address.

variables.name: unsubscribe_link
variables.description:
Prints the URL of the page that allows recipients to manage subscription preferences or unsubscribe from email communications. This variable should be used in the href attribute of an <a> anchor tag.

See Email content variables for additional unsubscribe_link_single and unsubscribe_link_all variables.

variables.name: background_color
variables.description: Background color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.background_color.

variables.name: body_border_color
variables.description: Body border color setting as a hex value. This option becomes available when you select Manually set email border color under the Border color options dropdown menu in settings. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.body_border_color.

variables.name: body_border_color_choice
variables.description: The value for this variable is controlled by the Border color options dropdown menu in settings. Values are: BORDER_AUTOMATIC, BORDER_MANUAL, BORDER_NONE. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.body_border_color_choice.

variables.name: body_color
variables.description: Body color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.body_color.

variables.name: primary_accent_color
variables.description: Primary accent color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.primary_accent_color.

variables.name: primary_font
variables.description: Primary font setting as a string. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.primary_font.

variables.name: primary_font_color
variables.description: Primary font color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.primary_font_color.

variables.name: primary_font_size
variables.description: Primary font size setting as a px value. For blog templates, use site_settings.primary_font_size.

variables.name: primary_font_size_num
variables.description: Primary font size number, excluding px.

variables.name: secondary_accent_color
variables.description: Secondary accent color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.secondary_accent_color.

variables.name: secondary_font
variables.description: Secondary font setting as a string. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.secondary_font.

variables.name: secondary_font_color
variables.description: Secondary font color setting as a hex value. To print this value in page or blog templates, use site_settings.secondary_font_color.

variables.name: secondary_font_size_num
variables.description: Secondary font size number, excluding px.

variables.name: content.create_page
variables.description: Will be True if there is a web page version of the email.

variables.name: content.email_body
variables.description: The main body of the email. This variable renders a rich text module.

variables.name: content.emailbody_plaintext
variables.description: The optional override of the plain text email body.

variables.name: content.from_name
variables.description: The from name of the email sender.

variables.name: content.reply_to
variables.description: The reply-to address for the email.

variables.name: content.subject
variables.description: The subject of the email.

variables.name: email_body_padding
variables.description: The email body padding setting.

variables.name: email_body_width
variables.description: The email body width setting.

variables.name: site_settings.company_street_address_2
variables.description: Prints the address line 2 from the account’s CAN-SPAM footer settings.

variables.name: site_settings.office_location_name
variables.description: Prints the office location name from the account’s CAN-SPAM footer settings.

variables.name: subscription_confirmation_url
variables.description: Prints the URL of the subscription preferences confirmation page. This URL is dynamically generated on send.

variables.name: subscription_name
variables.description: Prints the name of the email type specified for that email.

variables.name: unsubscribe_anchor
variables.description: Generates an anchor tag with the word “unsubscribe” linked to your unsubscribe page.

variables.name: unsubscribe_link_single
variables.description: Prints the URL of the page that allows recipients to unsubscribe from email communications for the individual subscription type of the sent email. This variable should be used in the href attribute of an <a> anchor tag.

variables.name: unsubscribe_link_all
variables.description: Prints the URL of the page that allows recipients to unsubscribe from email communications for all subscription types. This variable should be used in the href attribute of an <a> anchor tag.

variables.name: unsubscribe_section
variables.description: Renders an unsubscribe section that includes an unsubscribe link, as well as help text.

variables.name: view_as_page_section
variables.description: Generates a link with help text that leads to a webpage version of an email.

variables.name: view_as_page_url
variables.description: Generates a link that leads to a webpage version of an email.

variables.name: membership_company_name
variables.description: The company name, as set in the account’s private content settings.

variables.name: membership_domain
variables.description: The domain of the private content website.

variables.name: membership_logo_src
variables.description: The URL of the logo to display, as set in the account’s brand kit settings.

variables.name: membership_logo_alt
variables.description: The logo’s alt attribute, as set in the account’s brand kit settings.

variables.name: membership_registration_link
variables.description: Link to the registration page for the private content website.

variables.name: membership_password_saved_link
variables.description: Link to the password saved page. The link will redirect the visitor to a random restricted page that they have access to.

variables.name: membership_password_reset_link
variables.description: Link to the reset password page for the private content website.

variables.name: membership_passwordless_auth_link
variables.description: Link to the password-less member login page.

variables.name: membership_verify_registration_link
variables.description: Link for the registration verification page.

variables.name: membership_website_admin
variables.description: The name of the website admin, as set in the account’s private content settings.

variables.name: standard_footer_includes
variables.description: Renders the HubSpot tracking code and any other code included in your Footer HTML in Content Settings or the options of a particular page. This tag should be inserted directly before the closing body tag.

variables.name: standard_header_includes
variables.description: Adds jQuery, layout.css, any attached stylesheets, a meta viewport tag, Google Analytics tracking code, other page meta information, and code added to the head tag at the domain/template/page level. This variable should be added to the <head> of HTML templates.

variables.name: builtin_body_classes
variables.description: Dynamically prints helpful classes that differentiate the markup of content created with that template (i.e., type of content and content name). This makes styling different types of content or particular pages easier. This variable should be used in the class attribute of the body tag on coded templates.

variables.name: request_contact.is_logged_in
variables.description:
Whether or not the requesting contact is logged in to a website’s private content. The value of this variable will return true if the requesting contact is logged in, and false if the requesting contact has logged out. A contact can be logged out by directing them to https://<www.yourdomain.com>/_hcms/mem/logout using one of your connected domains.

Using this variable will disable page caching. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives.

variables.name: request_contact.list_memberships
variables.description: A dictionary of IDs that represents the lists of which the contact is a member. Using this variable will disable page caching. Learn more about page prerendering and JavaScript alternatives

variables.name: content.language
variables.description:
A dictionary of information about the language settings of a page.
content.language.languageTag returns the language identifier of a page (e.g., "en" or "es").
content.language.textDirection.value returns the text direction of the language of the page (e.g., "rtl" or "ltr").

variables.name: request.cookies
variables.description: A dictionary of cookie names mapped to cookie values. Using this variable will disable page caching.

variables.name: request.domain
variables.description: The domain used to access this page.

variables.name: request.full_url
variables.description: The URL used to access this page.

variables.name: request.path
variables.description: The path component of the URL.

variables.name: request.path_and_query
variables.description: The path and query component of the URL.

variables.name: request.query
variables.description: The query string component of the URL. request.query_dict automatically splits the query strings into key value pairs, and is recommended over the raw query for most use-cases.

variables.name: request.query_dict
variables.description: The query string converted into a name->value dictionary.

variables.name: request.referrer
variables.description: The HTTP referrer, the url of the page that linked to the current page. Using this variable will disable page caching.

variables.name: request.remote_ip
variables.description: The IP address of the visitor. Using this variable will disable page caching.

variables.name: request.scheme
variables.description: The protocol of the request (either http or https).

variables.name: request.search_engine
variables.description: The search engine used to find this page, if applicable (e.g., google, aol, live, yahoo, images.google).

variables.name: request.search_keyword
variables.description: The keyword phrase used to find this page, if applicable.

variables.name: request.headers
variables.description: A dictionary of available request headers. Usage of this variable will disable page caching in order to interpret individualized headers for each request.

variables.name: blog_author
variables.description:
This variable contains blog author information for blog author listing pages. It can be used to create conditional logic to render markup for blog author listings. It also contains the following properties:
blog_author.avatar
blog_author.bio
blog_author.display_name
blog_author.email
blog_author.facebook
blog_author.google_plus
blog_author.has_social_profiles
blog_author.linkedin
blog_author.twitter
blog_author.website

variables.name: content.blog_post_author
variables.description:
This variable contains individual blog post author information for a given post. Returns only the first author when multiple are assigned (use content.blog_author_list instead for multi-author support). It contains the following properties:
content.blog_post_author.avatar
content.blog_post_author.bio
content.blog_post_author.display_name
content.blog_post_author.email
content.blog_post_author.facebook
content.blog_post_author.google_plus
content.blog_post_author.has_social_profiles
content.blog_post_author.linkedin
content.blog_post_author.slug
content.blog_post_author.twitter
content.blog_post_author.website

variables.name: content.blog_author_list
variables.description:
A list of blog author objects assigned to a given post. Returns all authors, including a single author when only one is set. Use instead of content.blog_post_author when building templates that support multiple authors. Each object in the list contains the following properties:
avatar
bio
display_name

variables.name: blog
variables.description: An alias for group. For example, you can use either blog.absolute_url or group.absolute_url.

variables.name: content.comment_count
variables.description: The number of comments for the current blog post.

variables.name: content.comment_list
variables.description: A list of the comments for the current blog post.

variables.name: current_page_num
variables.description: The integer index of the current page of blog posts in the view.

variables.name: content.featured_image
variables.description: The source URL of the featured image, selected when the blog was published.

variables.name: content.featured_image_alt_text
variables.description: The alt text of the featured image.

variables.name: last_page_num
variables.description: The integer index of the last page of blog posts in the view.

variables.name: next_page_num
variables.description: The integer index of the next page of blog posts in the view.

variables.name: content.next_post_featured_image
variables.description: The URL of the featured image of the next blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.next_post_featured_image_alt_text
variables.description: Alt text for the next post’s featured image if alt text exists.

variables.name: content.next_post_name
variables.description: The name of the next blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.next_post_slug
variables.description: The URL slug of the next blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.post_body
variables.description: The body of the blog post.

variables.name: content.post_list_content
variables.description: The body blog post content, modified for the listing page. The final output is affected by the blog’s summary settings. If featured images are enabled in settings, this variable will remove any images above the read more separator automatically.

variables.name: content.post_list_summary_featured_image
variables.description: The featured image of post summaries to be used in the blog listing template, as set in blog settings.

variables.name: content.post_summary
variables.description: The blog post summary. This content is determined by the read more separator in the blog editor.

variables.name: content.previous_post_featured_image
variables.description: The URL of the featured image of the previous blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.previous_post_featured_image_alt_text
variables.description: Alt text for the previous post’s featured image if alt text exists.

variables.name: content.previous_post_name
variables.description: The name of the previous blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.previous_post_slug
variables.description: The URL slug of the previous blog post, if one exists.

variables.name: content.publish_date_localized
variables.description: A string representing the date/time when the blog post was published, formatted according to the blog’s language and date formatting settings.

variables.name: simple_list_page
variables.description: Whether the requested page is the ‘all posts’ page containing links to all blog posts.

variables.name: content.tag_list
variables.description: Can be used to render markup for listing blog tags by looping through it (e.g., {% for tag in content.tag_list %}).

variables.name: contents
variables.description: A sequence of your blog posts that are iterated through using a for loop, available on blog listing pages.

variables.name: contents.total_count
variables.description: Total number of posts in a listing (blog listing, topic listing, author listing).

variables.name: contents.total_page_count
variables.description: Total number of pages of posts based on your number of posts per page.

variables.name: contents_tags
variables.description: Get a list of all blog tags in the contents sequence of posts.

variables.name: group
variables.description: A dictionary containing variables that pertain to an entire blog.

variables.name: group.absolute_url
variables.description: The base URL of a blog.

variables.name: group.allow_comments
variables.description: Evaluates to true if comments are allowed.

variables.name: group.description
variables.description: The meta description of the blog, as set in blog settings. Used for the meta description on certain listing pages.

variables.name: group.header
variables.description: The header of the blog.

variables.name: group.html_title
variables.description: The title of this blog as it should appear in the <title> tag.

variables.name: group.id
variables.description: The unique ID of a blog. This ID can be found in the URL of the Blog Dashboard for a particular blog.

variables.name: group.language
variables.description: A dictionary containing information about a blog’s language. group.language.languageTag can be used in conditionals to render different content on the different language variations of a multi-language blog.

variables.name: group.public_title
variables.description: The title of this blog as it should appear at the top of rendered pages.

variables.name: group.show_summary_in_listing
variables.description: Whether post summaries are displayed on the blog listing page, as set in blog settings.

variables.name: group.slug
variables.description: The path to this blog.

variables.name: group.use_featured_image_in_summary
variables.description: Whether featured images are shown in post summaries, as set in blog settings.

variables.name: archive_list_page
variables.description: Evaluates to true if page is a blog archived page. Ex: https://www.example.com/blog/archive/2020/02 would return true.

variables.name: dynamic_page_crm_object
variables.description: The CRM object of the dynamic page that matches with the page request path. If the request is to the listing page, this value will be null.

variables.name: dynamic_page_crm_object_type_fqn
variables.description: The fully qualified name (FQN) of the crm object. The FQN is an assigned unique ID for the object, including the ID of your HubSpot account and the object name. The FQN can be used in the crm_objects function.

variables.name: dynamic_page_hubdb_table_id
variables.description: The ID of the table selected in the Advanced settings tab of the page editor.

variables.name: dynamic_page_hubdb_row
variables.description: The HubDB row of the dynamic page that matches with the page request path. If the request is to the listing page, this value will be null.

variables.name: row.hs_id
variables.description: The internal ID of a HubDB row.

variables.name: row.hs_name
variables.description: The name of the HubDB row.

variables.name: row.hs_path
variables.description: The path of the HubDB row. Used to resolve a request to one row in the table specified by dynamic_page_hubdb_table_id.

variables.name: row.hs_child_table_id
variables.description: The child table ID of the HubDB row. Can be used to build nested templates.

variables.name: row.hs_parent_row
variables.description: The parent row of the HubDB row. Can only be used when using child tables for nested templates.

variables.name: dynamic_page_route_level
variables.description: Current depth of a page in a multi-level dynamic template. The value starts at 0 and increments with each additional table layer.

variables.name: node.label
variables.description: The menu label of the page.

variables.name: node.url
variables.description: URL of the page.

variables.name: node.pageId
variables.description: ID of the page if within HubSpot.

variables.name: node.contentGroupId
variables.description: Blog ID of the page if it is a HubSpot blog post.

variables.name: node.parentNode
variables.description: The parent node of the current node. The parent node will have the current node in its children property.

variables.name: node.children
variables.description: The list of child nodes for the current node.

variables.name: node.activeBranch
variables.description: Evaluates to true if the node is in the top-level branch that the current page is in.

variables.name: node.activeNode
variables.description: Evaluates to true if the node is the current page.

variables.name: node.level
variables.description: The number of levels deep the current node is from the top-level nodes.

variables.name: node.pageTitle
variables.description: Name of the content page if within HubSpot.

variables.name: node.slug
variables.description: Path slug of the page.

variables.name: node.linkTarget
variables.description: Link target of the page.

variables.name: is_in_hs_app
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the HubSpot app.

variables.name: is_in_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within any content editor.

variables.name: is_in_global_content_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the global content editor.

variables.name: is_in_theme_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the theme editor.

variables.name: is_in_page_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the page editor.

variables.name: is_in_blog_post_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the blog post editor.

variables.name: is_in_email_editor
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the email editor.

variables.name: is_in_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within any preview context.

variables.name: is_in_theme_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the theme previewer.

variables.name: is_in_template_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the template previewer.

variables.name: is_in_page_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the page previewer.

variables.name: is_in_blog_post_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the blog post previewer.

variables.name: is_in_email_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the email previewer.

variables.name: is_in_module_previewer
variables.description: Returns true if content is being rendered within the module previewer.
